children.text: is assigned to, and serving in, a position included in the list established under for which recruitment or retention of personnel is difficult,section 1616i(b)(1) of this title
the Secretary determines is needed by the Service,

children.label: A
children.text: completed 3 years of employment with the Service, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any Federal education loan repayment program, and
enters into an agreement with the Service for continued employment for a period of not less than 1 year.

children.heading: Minimum award percentage to nurses
children.label: b Minimum award percentage to nurses
children.text: Beginning with fiscal year 1993, not less than 25 percent of the retention bonuses awarded each year under subsection (a) shall be awarded to nurses.

children.heading: Rates; maximum rate
children.label: c Rates; maximum rate
children.text: The Secretary may establish rates for the retention bonus which shall provide for a higher annual rate for multiyear agreements than for single year agreements referred to in subsection (a)(4), but in no event shall the annual rate be more than $25,000 per annum.

children.heading: Time of payment
children.label: d Time of payment
children.text: The retention bonus for the entire period covered by the agreement described in subsection (a)(4) shall be paid at the beginning of the agreed upon term of service.

children.heading: Refund; interest
children.label: e Refund; interest
children.text: Any physician or nurse failing to complete the agreed upon term of service, except where such failure is through no fault of the individual, shall be obligated to refund to the Government the full amount of the retention bonus for the period covered by the agreement, plus interest as determined by the Secretary in accordance with section 1616a()(2)(B) of this title.l

children.heading: Physicians and nurses employed under Indian Self-Determination Act
children.label: f Physicians and nurses employed under Indian Self-Determination Act

children.text: a position for which recruitment or retention is difficult; and
necessary for providing health care services to Indians.
